lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Look, here come 'nother wan'be jubbly chaser.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Be nice, Gurt, or you'll scare off another one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Another what? What are you two doing here?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Me's big boss of of dis boatsie skill test. Call it Jubbly Jive. Only best wiv boatsies can hunt da jubblies.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Gurtob is da bestest jubbly hunter in all of da seas. No one surpass Gurtob.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Boatsies? Jubblies? Skill test? What's going on here.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: I'll take it from here, Gurt.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Dis... Sorry, this corner of the Shrouded Ocean is where we Barracudas like to challenge ourselves to a fun Barracuda Trial called the Jubbly Jive.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: While he may not look it, Gurtob here is actually an expert sailor and trial master for the Barracudas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An ogre is a trial master for the Barracudas? That's surprising.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Not at all! He may be rough around the edges and difficult to understand at times, but he's a fantastic sailor and that's the only thing us Barracudas care about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So what do I do here? What's the challenge?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Creatures talk... talk... long. Go da fastest, grab da toadies, throw da toadies, trick da jubblies, eat da jubblies. Dat's it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I follow.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Weak creature needs more boatsie skills first.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Yes, you'd probably benefit from testing your sailing skills elsewhere before attempting this trial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice. I'll bear that in mind.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: The goal of this trial is to use balloon toads to lure jubblies. First up, you'll want to sail west to collect da... sorry, collect the balloon toads from a boat of ours.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: On your way, keep an eye out for any lost supplies. You'll need to collect all of them to complete the trial.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Sailing over rapids will give you a burst of speed! Try to go over as many as you can. The more rapids you go through in quick succession, the faster you'll go!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: As you continue sailing west, keep an eye out for the jubblies. Remembering where they are will help you later in the trial. They tend to be perched on top of rocky outcrops or trees growing out of the water.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: The total number of them will depend on the rank you're going for. They're also fairly consistent, so your experience should be the same during each trial attempt.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Once you've sailed far enough west, you should be able to spot the boat where you can collect the balloon toads from. The maximum amount you can collect depends on the rank you're going for.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Once you've collected some balloon toads, you'll want to start sailing back this way so you can begin luring the jubblies.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: As you sail, watch out for the extra thick stagnant patches of water. They'll slow down any boat, so do your best to avoid them.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: As you keep making your way back, you'll want to start luring the jubblies back with you. The goal is to lure all of them back to us here.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: To lure a jubbly, look out for empty outcrops similar to the ones they like to perch on. If you throw a balloon toad at one of these outcrops, a nearby jubbly will move to it.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: It's worth noting that jubblies will only travel to the closest outcrop to them, so you'll need to lure them one outcrop at a time. Make sure to keep track of where you've thrown toads and where the jubblies started.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: When attempting higher ranks, you may want to set up balloon toads at multiple outcrops ahead of time!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: While the jubblies are important, don't forget to keep collecting the lost supplies as you come back as well.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: If you can lure all the jubblies and collect all the lost supplies in the time given, you'll be awarded a Barracuda rank! Each trial has three ranks: Swordfish, Shark or Marlin. Each rank is harder than the last.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: For this trial, you'll be asked to lure additional jubblies and collect even more lost supplies as the rank increases.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: You can't attempt the next rank until you beat the one before it. You can also try unranked if you'd like to warm up beforehand.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: See, is like Gurtob said so. Go da fastest, grab da toadies, throw da toadies, trick da jubblies, eat da jubblies. Dat's it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Do you have any tips or tricks for me?

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: First up, you're going to need a boat with at least a mithril helm in order to withstand the strong rapids here, but don't forget, provided you can withstand them, they'll give you a burst of speed!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Secondly, you really want to try to avoid the fetid pools around us. They won't harm you or your boat, but they will completely kill all momentum you've built up.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: The only way to get across them is by using a wind catcher, but even then the timing is tricky, so we only give those out to sailors who have achieved at least Shark rank here.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Thirdly, trim your sails frequently to build up even more speed, but be careful! While it's important to go fast, doing so can make it harder to navigate around the fetid pools.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: If I were you, I'd consider briefly lowering your speed for those tricky turns to avoid getting your boat stuck.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Hah! Gurtob sail never go down. Only go fastest.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Finally, and you probably don't need to be told, but watch out for those rocks, hitting them will put a stop to all of your momentum, just like the pools.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice. I'll bear that in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to attempt the Jubbly Jive.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: You should make sure your helm is manned before starting the trial.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: For this Barracuda Trial you are not allowed to bring any crewmates.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me transfer my crew away?

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Yes, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Jubbly Jive?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Dis wat Gurtob best at! Dancin' wif da jubblies.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: He's right, you know. Whilst exploring the ocean, a group of Barracudas saw Gurtob 'sailing' around this place on what could barely be described as a boat, hunting jubblies.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Dey join in and try steal Gurtob jub jubs! But creatures nice and give Gurtob strong boatsie. Now Gurtob friends.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Gurtob friends indeed! When the Barracudas tried it themselves, they realised how much fun it was sailing around these tight spaces. They worked with Gurtob to design this trial!

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Gurtob make bestest trial back then! Now yous little creatures make it easier, but me think it still best.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: It's true that some claim the trial is easier now than before due to new pathways being carved through the fetid pools. Still, don't let that fool you! The course is still a tricky one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I need to do during the trial?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Go da fastest, grab da toadies, throw da toadies, trick da jubblies, eat da jubblies. Dat's it.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Let me explain it in non-Gurtob terms...

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: The goal of this trial is to use balloon toads to lure jubblies. First up, you'll want to sail west to collect da... sorry, collect the balloon toads from a boat of ours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Me's Gurtob. Da fastest jubbly hunter on boatsies and big boss of dis boatsie skill test.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: And I'm Ros. I'm fairly new to the Barracudas and I help Gurtob out with managing this trial.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: He's not much of a speaker and is easily misunderstood, so I help translate and make sure that any up and coming prospective Barracudas understand exactly what they need to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why stay out here in this awful smelling place? Aren't there better places to be at sea?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Dis Gurtob home. Only funny smelly is you's scrawny creatures.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Gurtob find funny when you's creatures gets stucks in bubbly watery. Gurtob also likes when you's creatures bring da jubblies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I get what's in it for him, but what's in it for you, Ros?

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Well, after joining the Barracudas, I discovered a fascination for different cultures. This is just a small step in my personal journey to understand more about the world!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Plus da jubblies do taste good!

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Da jubblies do taste da bestest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are the Barracudas?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Dey like da creatures which sound like da fishies but no taste like da fishes but fast like da fastest fishies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm...

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Gurtob sure has a way with words!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Us Barracudas are the fastest sailors across the entire ocean! Our motto is 'Anyone from anywhere as long as they can sail.'

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: We work together to help hone each other's skills and test our mettle through challenges called Barracuda Trials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I become a Barracuda?

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Of course you can! There's a bunch of trial masters like Gurtob scattered across the seas. Best our trials and earn your spot in our ranks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd best be off.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Look, here come 'nother wan'be jubbly chaser.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Be nice, Gurt, or you'll scare off another one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any tips or tricks for me?

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: First up, you're going to need a boat with at least a mithril helm in order to withstand the strong rapids here, but don't forget, provided you can withstand them, they'll give you a burst of speed!

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Secondly, you really want to try to avoid the fetid pools around us. They won't harm you or your boat, but they will completely kill all momentum you've built up.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: The only way to get across them is by using a wind catcher, but even then the timing is tricky, so we only give those out to sailors who have achieved at least Shark rank here.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Thirdly, trim your sails frequently to build up even more speed, but be careful! While it's important to go fast, doing so can make it harder to navigate around the fetid pools.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: If I were you, I'd consider briefly lowering your speed for those tricky turns to avoid getting your boat stuck.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Hah! Gurtob sail never go down. Only go fastest.

lines.speaker: Ros
lines.text: Finally, and you probably don't need to be told, but watch out for those rocks, hitting them will put a stop to all of your momentum, just like the pools.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice. I'll bear that in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I have any rewards to claim?

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Gurtob no have re... rewa... prize for creature.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: Das all re... rewa... prize Gurtob has for creature.

lines.speaker: Gurtob
lines.text: You go away! Busy eatin' da jubblies. Come back when belly full.
